mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, invalidating the negative earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in >70% of observations during tightening phases, breaking the supply-demand link.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate forces aggressive monetary tightening, which sharply increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS rises, with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer expenditure capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.05 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction or near-zero growth in real GDP indicates severe economic slowdown, which triggers recession fears and forces investors to de-risk equity portfolios, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below the 1.0% threshold, negating the recession fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and payroll employment signal synchronized economic expansion, which boosts market confidence and generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio for 5-day forward returns over any 3-year out-of-sample period.
